message:
chore(am): prefer pre-built release binary over cargo run

The `am` launcher script now checks for a compiled release binary at
\$CARGO_TARGET_DIR/release/mcp-agent-mail (or target/release/) and
execs it directly when found, avoiding the ~2-3s cargo overhead on
every server start. Falls back to `cargo run --release` when no
binary exists.

This also switches the fallback from debug to release mode, since
the TUI and HTTP server benefit substantially from optimized builds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -190,4 +190,10 @@ fi
 cd "$repo_root"
 echo "am: host=${host} port=${port} path=${resolved_path} tui=$([[ "$no_tui" -eq 0 ]] && echo on || echo off) rich=${LOG_RICH_ENABLED}" >&2
 
-exec cargo run -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host "$host" --port "$port" --path "$resolved_path" "${tui_flag[@]}" "${extra_args[@]}"
+# Use release binary if available, fall back to cargo run
+binary="${CARGO_TARGET_DIR:-${repo_root}/target}/release/mcp-agent-mail"
+if [[ -x "$binary" ]]; then
+  exec "$binary" serve --host "$host" --port "$port" --path "$resolved_path" "${tui_flag[@]}" "${extra_args[@]}"
+else
+  exec cargo run --release -p mcp-agent-mail -- serve --host "$host" --port "$port" --path "$resolved_path" "${tui_flag[@]}" "${extra_args[@]}"
+fi
